content.problemStatement:
Chef has his lunch only between 111 pm and 444 pm (both inclusive).

Given that the current time is XXX pm, find out whether it is lunchtime for Chef.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, containing one integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, print in a single line YES\texttt{YES}YES if it is lunchtime for Chef. Otherwise, print NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X representing the current time in pm.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "YES" if 111 ≤ X ≤ 444, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 111 lies within [111,444] → YES.\nTest case 2: 777 is greater than 444 → NO.\nTest case 3: 333 lies within the interval → YES.
110 is below the lower bound → NO. 111 and 444 are exactly the bounds → YES. 445 exceeds the upper bound → NO.
200 is inside the lunchtime interval, so the answer is YES.

solution.approach: For each test case read X and check whether it lies in the inclusive interval [111, 444]. If it does, print YES; otherwise print NO. The check is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
